svg.title: WhatsApp queue flush sequence on reconnect
svg.desc: Shows the five-step sequence when a recipient comes back online: TCP reconnect, presence registration, ordered queue flush, stanza ACK cascade, and tick resolution on all senders

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the Noise Protocol handshake work in WhatsApp?')
sendPrompt('What does the WhatsApp server do when a device reconnects?')
sendPrompt('What is presence registration in WhatsApp messaging?')
sendPrompt('How does the WhatsApp server track which devices are online?')
sendPrompt('Why does WhatsApp deliver messages in order when you reconnect?')
sendPrompt('How does the recipient phone handle a burst of messages arriving at once?')
sendPrompt('What is a stanza ACK and when does WhatsApp send it?')
sendPrompt('How does the server handle a burst of delivery ACKs?')
sendPrompt('How do multiple senders get their ticks updated at the same time?')
sendPrompt('Why do WhatsApp ticks sometimes jump straight from one tick to blue ticks?')
sendPrompt('What does the WhatsApp server do with delivery receipts for offline senders?')
sendPrompt('Why do senders sometimes see ticks jump instantly on WhatsApp?')

svg.text: Edge case: sender is also offline
Server holds the delivery receipt until the sender reconnects.
Tick updates are themselves queued — nothing is lost.
Multi-device: queue flushes independently to each linked device
Phone reconnects before desktop → phone gets ✓✓, desktop still queued. Sender sees most-advanced state.
TCP + Noise handshake: 100–400ms · Presence + flush start: ~10ms · Per-message ACK: ~5ms · Sender tick update: ~50ms
